description:
A mobile build engineer has asked for help scheduling two maintenance tasks on their continuous-integration runner.

1. Every night at 02:30 the Android build script located at  
   /home/user/build/build_android.sh  
   must be executed and its console output appended (not overwritten) to  
   /home/user/build/build_android.log

2. Every Sunday at 04:00 the artefact-cleanup script located at  
   /home/user/build/cleanup_artifacts.sh  
   must be executed and its console output appended (not overwritten) to  
   /home/user/build/cleanup.log

Your goal is to automate these two jobs using the current user’s personal crontab (i.e. with the command `crontab -e` or its batch equivalent).  
Do NOT touch /etc/crontab or any system-wide cron directory; use only the user crontab.

Verification requirements  
After you have installed the two cron entries, create a text file named  
/home/user/cron_setup.log  
containing exactly two lines (no extra spaces or blank lines):

BUILD JOB OK  
CLEANUP JOB OK

The automated test suite will check two things:

A. The user’s crontab contains both of the following **exact lines** (spacing and redirection tokens must match exactly).  
   30 2 * * * /home/user/build/build_android.sh >> /home/user/build/build_android.log 2>&1  
   0 4 * * 0 /home/user/build/cleanup_artifacts.sh >> /home/user/build/cleanup.log 2>&1  

B. The file /home/user/cron_setup.log exists and its content is exactly what is shown above, including capitalisation and the newline separating the two lines.

Hints  
• Make sure the paths in the cron entries are absolute.  
• Use “>>” so logs are appended each run.  
• The order of the two lines inside the crontab does not matter, but they both must be present verbatim.  
• Remember that each line in cron has the format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week command.  
• Do not remove any pre-existing cron jobs that might already be present for the user.

The task is complete when the two cron entries are in place and /home/user/cron_setup.log matches the required content.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

Directory /home/user/build already exists.

Files present in that directory with the following exact contents and permissions (755):

1. /home/user/build/build_android.sh  
   #!/usr/bin/env bash  
   echo "Building Android..."

2. /home/user/build/cleanup_artifacts.sh  
   #!/usr/bin/env bash  
   echo "Cleaning up old artefacts..."

No user crontab is installed at task start (i.e. `crontab -l` would exit with code 1 and print “no crontab for user”).

Expected final state:

1. The output of `crontab -l` (after the agent’s work) must contain BOTH of the following literal lines, exactly once each:

30 2 * * * /home/user/build/build_android.sh >> /home/user/build/build_android.log 2>&1  
0 4 * * 0 /home/user/build/cleanup_artifacts.sh >> /home/user/build/cleanup.log 2>&1

Spacing: single spaces separating the six fields (the five schedule fields plus the full command string).  
No leading or trailing spaces on either line.

2. The file /home/user/cron_setup.log exists and contains exactly (with Unix line endings):

BUILD JOB OK\n  
CLEANUP JOB OK\n

3. File permissions: /home/user/cron_setup.log must be readable (0644) and owned by user.

The presence of any extra content in the user’s crontab or in /home/user/cron_setup.log will cause the automated verification to fail.
